Make sure you create a virtual environment , activate it, and then install it, for example:
tutorial/testing::section-0002::admoniti.section_text:
To use TestClient , first install httpx .

Make sure you create a virtual environment , activate it, and then install it, for example:

section_text_parts: Notice that the testing functions are normal def , not async def .
And the calls to the client are also normal calls, not using await .
This allows you to use pytest directly without complications.
tutorial/testing::section-0002::admoniti.section_text:
Notice that the testing functions are normal def , not async def .

And the calls to the client are also normal calls, not using await .

This allows you to use pytest directly without complications.

tutorial/testing::section-0002::admoniti.title: Technical Details
section_text_parts: You could also use from starlette.testclient import TestClient .
FastAPI provides the same starlette.testclient as fastapi.testclient just as a convenience for you, the developer. But it comes directly from Starlette.
tutorial/testing::section-0002::admoniti.section_text:
You could also use from starlette.testclient import TestClient .

FastAPI provides the same starlette.testclient as fastapi.testclient just as a convenience for you, the developer. But it comes directly from Starlette.

section_text_parts: If you want to call async functions in your tests apart from sending requests to your FastAPI application (e.g. asynchronous database functions), have a look at the Async Tests in the advanced tutorial.
tutorial/testing::section-0002::admoniti.section_text: If you want to call async functions in your tests apart from sending requests to your FastAPI application (e.g. asynchronous database functions), have a look at the Async Tests in the advanced tutorial.

section_text_parts: Note that the TestClient receives data that can be converted to JSON, not Pydantic models.
If you have a Pydantic model in your test and you want to send its data to the application during testing, you can use the jsonable_encoder described in JSON Compatible Encoder .
tutorial/testing::section-0008::admoniti.section_text:
Note that the TestClient receives data that can be converted to JSON, not Pydantic models.

If you have a Pydantic model in your test and you want to send its data to the application during testing, you can use the jsonable_encoder described in JSON Compatible Encoder .
